title: A simple and fast single step method to isolate and stain the CD27 B cells (plasma cells) with quantum dots magnetic beads antibody conjugate for fluorescent microscopy
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ekpnv","text":"Background Today, mononuclear cells are stained in a two-step process: in the ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"89t7s","text":"first step, the cells are isolated, and in the second step, they are stained. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5m7f5","text":"This two-step process takes a long time before the cells can be observed under ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dr1fg","text":"a fluorescence microscope. In addition, quantum dots have further advantages ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cvnl4","text":"over conventional dyes such as FITC, Cy3, Cy5, etc., as they do not fade and emit ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fi7es","text":"fluorescence in a narrow range. We decided to develop a new method in which ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"36j6n","text":"the cells can be isolated and stained in a single step using quantum dot magnetic bead ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fn87h","text":"antibody conjugates for CD27-positive B cells.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"evq15","text":" Material and Methods CD27 cells from mononuclear cell cultures were stained and ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":1,"length":20}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3d02q","text":"isolated simultaneously in magnetic racks using quantum dot magnetic bead antibody ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4k4j5","text":"conjugates with two different wavelengths (510 and 700 nm). They were then observed ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"h291","text":"under a fluorescence microscope.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dut5a","text":" Results CD27 cells were successfully isolated and stained within 30 minutes, emitting ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":1,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9lfn1","text":"a specific fluorescence under the microscope so that only the specific CD27 B cells were","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"car5h","text":"observed and no other cells were present. Under the normal microscope, the magnetic beads\nadhered to their surfaces.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fetne","text":"Conclusions This may be the first report of the successful development of a rapid one-step ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4l56h","text":"method for isolating and staining CD27 B cells from mononuclear cells using quantum dot ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c83c6","text":"magnetic bead antibody conjugates within 30 minutes, compared to the two-step method, ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7vmsg","text":"which takes more than an hour. This method opens up new possibilities for immunotherapies and\nclinical applications.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"43rh0","text":"Please read the protocol properly before start of the experiment.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"mve8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5u8mb","text":"1. Please use a high-quality fluorescence microscope, as inferior and cheap microscopes do not deliver results and are therefore a waste of time.\n\n2. Please keep away your mobile telephone and camera from the magnetic rack as the magnet can damage your display. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a5g8u","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6gj6c","text":"3. Work under laminar flow.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"a5f49","text":"Quantum dots magnetic beads antibody conjugate (MICROBOSS Nanomedicine GmbH, Germany)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"852es","text":"Magnetic racks (Genekam Biotechnology AG, Germany)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9ahqf","text":"PBS (Washing buffer, Lonza, USA)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8p9nu","text":"Fluorescent microscope (Zeiss, Germany)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b1hfe","text":"50 ml sterile tubes ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bs2n","text":"Microscopic slides (Thermofischer, Germany)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"49bn6","text":"5 ml tubes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Centrifuge 5 ml of cell culture of human mononuclear cells at 18000 rpm and wash twice with PBS. Collect the pellet.
Add 2 μl of quantum dot magnetic bead antibody conjugates (510 nm and 700 nm) on the pellet and incubate it in the dark for 10 minutes with intermittent mixing.
Wash the cells with 5 ml PBS while placing on a magnetic rack for 3 minutes. Remove the fluid while keeping pellet in the tube.
Suspend the pellet in 100 μl PBS; apply 1 μl to a slide. Let it dry and fix in ethanol for 5 minutes, and mount it the slide.

Observe the CD27 poisitive cells using a Zeiss fluorescence microscope with UV, blue, and green filters at various magnifications, with and without oil immersion. Attempts using a lower-cost Biobase microscope were unsuccessful. Do not waste your time using low quality microscope.
Inocubate the 5 ml of cell culture with 2 μl of quantum dot magnetic bead antibody conjugate for 10 minutes.
Suspend the cells in 5 ml on a magnetic rack, discard the supernatant and wash pellet twice with 5 ml PBS.
Apply 2 μl of the pellet after suspending it in 100 µl PBS to a slide. Let it dry, fix it, and mount it.

Observe the CD27 positive cells under a Zeiss fluorescence microscope with UV, blue, and green filters at various magnifications, with and without oil immersion. Attempts using a lower-cost Biobase microscope were unsuccessful.
creator: Sudhir Bhatia
